description_long: This is a robust, single-bladed axe with a long, straight handle and a distinctive wedge-shaped head. The blade is broad and slightly curved, with a pronounced edge for effective chopping, while the opposite side features a pointed poll, likely for piercing or splitting tasks. The handle appears to be cylindrical and tapers slightly towards the end, providing a secure grip for two-handed use. The head is mounted perpendicularly to the handle, with no visible fasteners, suggesting a traditional wedged mounting style. The entire object is a uniform dark color, giving it a utilitarian and possibly modern appearance. No visible markings, engravings, or labels are present on the surface.
description: A dark, single-bladed axe with a long straight handle and a wedge-shaped head featuring a broad blade and pointed poll.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the wedge-shaped head and the full length of the straight handle.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the broad, curved blade and the pointed poll opposite the blade.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, again showing the wedge-shaped head and handle alignment.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the poll and the back of the blade, with the handle extending away.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical handle, likely wood, with a smooth surface and slight taper for grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad, slightly curved blade with a sharp edge and a pointed poll on the opposite side; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the head fitted perpendicularly onto the handle.
